sentences.sentence: The changes that have caused the most disagreement are those in pronunciation.
sentences.translation: 引发最多争议的变化，是语音方面的变化。

structure.text: The changes
that have caused the most disagreement
are
those in pronunciation

difficult_points: that 引导定语从句，修饰 The changes，说明是哪些变化引发了争议。
those 指代 changes，those in pronunciation 即“语音方面的变化”。

sentences.sentence: We have various sources of evidence for the pronunciations of earlier times, such as the spellings, the treatment of words borrowed from other languages or borrowed by them, the descriptions of contemporary grammarians and spelling-reformers, and the modern pronunciations in all the languages and dialects concerned
sentences.translation: 关于早期语音，我们掌握着多种证据来源，比如拼写、本族语向其他语言借词或被其他语言借用时的处理方式、当时语法学家和拼写改革者的记述，以及所涉及的所有语言和方言中现今的发音。

structure.text: We
have various sources of evidence for the pronunciations of earlier times

such as the spellings, the treatment of words borrowed from other languages or borrowed by them, the descriptions of contemporary grammarians and spelling-reformers, and the modern pronunciations in all the languages and dialects concerned
borrowed from other languages or borrowed by them
concerned

difficult_points: such as... 列举早期语音证据的四种主要来源。
borrowed from other languages or borrowed by them 是过去分词短语，修饰 words，说明词汇是“从其他语言借来的”或“被其他语言借走的”双向借用关系。
concerned 是过去分词后置修饰 languages and dialects，意为“所涉及的”。
此句末尾原文缺少句号，与下一句连写。

sentences.sentence: From the middle of the sixteenth century, there are in England writers who attempt to describe the position of the speech-organs for the production of English phonemes, and who invent what are in effect systems of phonetic symbols.
sentences.translation: 从16世纪中叶起，英格兰出现了一些作家，他们尝试描述发出英语音位时发音器官的位置，并发明出实质上可称为音标系统的东西。

structure.text: From the middle of the sixteenth century
there are in England writers
who attempt to describe the position of the speech-organs for the production of English phonemes
to describe the position of the speech-organs
and who invent what are in effect systems of phonetic symbols
what are in effect systems of phonetic symbols

difficult_points: there are in England writers 是存在句的倒装形式，正常语序为 there are writers in England。
who... 和 who... 是两个并列的定语从句，共同修饰 writers。
what are in effect systems of phonetic symbols 是 invent 的宾语从句，what 相当于“……的东西”，in effect 意为“实质上、事实上”。

sentences.sentence: These various kinds of evidence, combined with a knowledge of the mechanisms of speech-production, can often give us a very good idea of the pronunciation of an earlier age, though absolute certainty is never possible.
sentences.translation: 这些不同种类的证据，结合对发音机制的了解，往往能让我们对早期的语音有相当清楚的认识，尽管绝对的确定性是永远无法达到的。

structure.text: These various kinds of evidence
combined with a knowledge of the mechanisms of speech-production
can often give us a very good idea of the pronunciation of an earlier age
though absolute certainty is never possible

difficult_points: combined with... 是过去分词短语，插入主语和谓语之间，修饰 evidence，说明证据要结合语音生成机制的知识。
give us a very good idea of 表示“让我们对……有清楚的认识”。
though 引导让步状语从句，说明尽管证据充分，也无法达到绝对确定。

expressions.text: give sb a good idea of
expressions.meaning_zh: 让某人对……有清楚的认识

sentences.sentence: When we study the pronunciation of a language over any period of a few generations or more, we find there are always large-scale regularities in the changes: for example, over a certain period of time, just about all the long [a:] vowels in a language may change into long [e:]vowels, or all the [b] consonants in a certain position (for example at the end of a word) may change into [p] consonants.
sentences.translation: 当我们考察一种语言在几代人以上的时间跨度内的语音变化时，我们会发现这些变化中总是存在大规模的规律：例如，在某一段时期内，一种语言里几乎所有的长元音[a:]都可能变成长元音[e:]，或者某一特定位置（比如词末）的所有辅音[b]都可能变成[p]。

structure.text: When we study the pronunciation of a language over any period of a few generations or more
we
study the pronunciation of a language
we
find
there are always large-scale regularities in the changes
for example, over a certain period of time, just about all the long [a:] vowels in a language may change into long [e:]vowels
or all the [b] consonants in a certain position (for example at the end of a word) may change into [p] consonants

difficult_points: When 引导时间状语从句，说明观察语音变化的时间跨度。
find 后省略了 that，接宾语从句 there are always large-scale regularities...。
冒号后的内容用具体例子说明前面所说的“大规模规律”，两个 or 连接的例子并列。

sentences.sentence: Such regular changes are often called sound laws.
sentences.translation: 这类有规律的变化常被称为“音变规律”。

structure.text: Such regular changes
are often called sound laws

difficult_points: are called 是被动语态，表示“被称为”。
sound laws 是语言学术语，指语音演变的规律。

sentences.sentence: There are no universal sound laws (even though sound laws often reflect universal tendencies), but simply particular sound laws for one given language (or dialect) at one given period
sentences.translation: 并不存在放之四海而皆准的音变规律（尽管音变规律常常体现出普遍性的趋势），有的只是针对某一特定语言（或方言）在某一特定时期适用的特定规律。

structure.text: There are no universal sound laws
even though sound laws often reflect universal tendencies
but simply particular sound laws for one given language (or dialect) at one given period

difficult_points: even though 引导让步状语从句，插在括号中，承认音变规律常反映普遍趋势，但仍强调其特殊性。
but 后省略了 there are，与前面的 There are no universal sound laws 并列对比。
此句末尾原文缺少句号，为该段末句。

sentences.sentence: It is also possible that fashion plays a part in the process of change.
sentences.translation: 同样有可能的是，时尚风气在语言变化的过程中也起着一定作用。

structure.text: It
is also possible
that fashion plays a part in the process of change

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，真正的主语是后面 that 引导的从句。
play a part in 表示“在……中起作用”。

sentences.sentence: It certainly plays a part in the spread of change: one person imitates another, and people with the most prestige are most likely to be imitated, so that a change that takes place in one social group may be imitated(more or less accurately) by speakers in another group.
sentences.translation: 时尚风气在变化的传播过程中确实起着作用：一个人模仿另一个人，而最有威望的人最有可能被模仿，因此某一社会群体中发生的变化，可能会（或多或少准确地）被另一群体中的说话者模仿。

structure.text: It
certainly plays a part in the spread of change
one person imitates another
and people with the most prestige are most likely to be imitated
so that a change that takes place in one social group may be imitated(more or less accurately) by speakers in another group
that takes place in one social group

difficult_points: 冒号后的内容具体说明时尚风气如何在传播中发挥作用。
are most likely to be imitated 是被动语态，说明最具威望的人最容易被模仿。
so that 引导结果状语从句，说明模仿链条最终导致的结果。
that takes place in one social group 是定语从句，修饰 a change。

expressions.text: be most likely to do sth

sentences.sentence: When a social group goes up or down in the world, its pronunciation of Russian, which had formerly been considered desirable, became on the contrary an undesirable kind of accent to have, so that people tried to disguise it.
sentences.translation: 当某一社会群体的地位上升或下降时，它原本被认为值得拥有的那种俄语口音，反而变成了一种不受欢迎的口音，以至于人们试图掩饰它。

structure.text: When a social group goes up or down in the world
its pronunciation of Russian
which had formerly been considered desirable
became on the contrary an undesirable kind of accent to have
so that people tried to disguise it

difficult_points: When 引导时间状语从句，说明社会群体地位变化的背景。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，修饰 its pronunciation of Russian，说明这种口音以前被视为值得拥有。
on the contrary 表示“相反”，与前面的“曾被认为值得拥有”形成对比。
so that 引导结果状语从句，说明口音变得不受欢迎后人们的反应。

expressions.text: go up or down in the world

sentences.sentence: Some of the changes in accepted English pronunciation in the seventeenth and eighteenth centuries have been shown to consist in the replacement of one style of pronunciation by another style already existing, and it is likely that such substitutions were a result of the great social changes of the period: the increased power and wealth of the middle classes, and their steady infiltration upwards into the ranks of the landed gentry, probably carried elements of middle-class pronunciation into upper-class speech.
sentences.translation: 十七、十八世纪英语规范发音中的部分变化，已被证明其实质是一种发音风格被另一种已经存在的发音风格所取代，而这类替代很可能是当时重大社会变迁的结果：中产阶级权势和财富的增长，以及他们不断向上渗透进入乡绅阶层的过程，很可能把中产阶级发音的一些元素带入了上层阶级的言语之中。

structure.text: Some of the changes in accepted English pronunciation in the seventeenth and eighteenth centuries
have been shown to consist in the replacement of one style of pronunciation by another style already existing
to consist in the replacement of one style of pronunciation by another style already existing
and it is likely
that such substitutions were a result of the great social changes of the period
the increased power and wealth of the middle classes, and their steady infiltration upwards into the ranks of the landed gentry
probably carried elements of middle-class pronunciation into upper-class speech

difficult_points: have been shown to consist in 是被动语态，表示“已被证明其实质在于……”。
it 是形式主语，真正的主语是 that 引导的从句。
冒号后的内容具体说明前文所说的“重大社会变迁”，其主语是 the increased power and wealth... and their steady infiltration...，两个并列名词短语共用谓语 carried。

sentences.sentence: A less specific variant of the argument is that the imitation of children is imperfect: they copy their parents' speech, but never reproduce it exactly.
sentences.translation: 该论点还有一种不太具体的说法，即儿童的模仿并不完美：他们模仿父母的言语，但从不能完全准确地复现。

structure.text: A less specific variant of the argument
is
that the imitation of children is imperfect
they copy their parents' speech
but never reproduce it exactly

difficult_points: that 引导表语从句，说明这一论点变体的具体内容。
冒号后的内容进一步解释儿童模仿“不完美”具体指什么。

sentences.sentence: This is true, but it is also true that such deviations from adult speech are usually corrected in later childhood.
sentences.translation: 这一点确实成立，但同样成立的是，这类偏离成人言语的现象通常会在儿童后期得到纠正。

structure.text: This is true
but it is also true
that such deviations from adult speech are usually corrected in later childhood

difficult_points: it 是形式主语，真正的主语是 that 引导的从句。
are usually corrected 是被动语态，说明这些偏离通常会被纠正。

sentences.sentence: Perhaps it is more significant that even adults show a certain amount of random variation in their pronunciation of a given phoneme, even if the phonetic context is kept unchanged.
sentences.translation: 或许更值得注意的是，即使语音语境保持不变，成年人在发某个音位时也会表现出一定程度的随机变化。

structure.text: it
is more significant
that even adults show a certain amount of random variation in their pronunciation of a given phoneme
even if the phonetic context is kept unchanged

difficult_points: it 是形式主语，真正的主语是 that 引导的从句。
even if 引导让步状语从句，说明即便语境不变，仍会出现随机变化。

sentences.sentence: This, however, cannot explain changes in pronunciation unless it can be shown that there is some systematic trend in the failures of imitation: if they are merely random deviations they will cancel one another out and there will be no net change in the language.
sentences.translation: 然而，这一点无法解释语音的变化，除非能够证明模仿失误中存在某种系统性的趋势：如果这些失误仅仅是随机的偏差，它们就会相互抵消，语言中就不会出现净变化。

structure.text: This
however
cannot explain changes in pronunciation
unless it can be shown that there is some systematic trend in the failures of imitation
that there is some systematic trend in the failures of imitation
if they are merely random deviations
they will cancel one another out
and there will be no net change in the language

difficult_points: unless 引导条件状语从句，it can be shown that... 中 it 是形式主语。
冒号后的内容用 if 引导的条件句，进一步解释“系统性趋势”缺失时会发生什么。
cancel one another out 表示“相互抵消”。

sentences.sentence: One such force which is often invoked is the principle of ease, or minimization of effort.
sentences.translation: 其中一种常被援引的力量，是“省力原则”，也就是使努力最小化。

structure.text: One such force
which is often invoked
is
the principle of ease, or minimization of effort

difficult_points: which 引导定语从句，修饰 One such force，说明这种力量常被人们提及援引。
or minimization of effort 是对 the principle of ease 的同位补充说明，即“省力原则”也可称为“努力最小化”。

sentences.sentence: The change from fussy to fuzzy would be an example of assimilation, which is a very common kind of change.
sentences.translation: 从fussy变为fuzzy的这种变化，可算作一个同化现象的例子，而同化是一种非常常见的变化类型。

structure.text: The change from fussy to fuzzy
would be an example of assimilation
which is a very common kind of change

difficult_points: which 引导非限制性定语从句，修饰 assimilation，补充说明同化是一种常见的变化类型。
would be 带有推测举例的语气，表示一种举例性的判断。

sentences.sentence: Assimilation is the changing of a sound under the influence of a neighbouring one.
sentences.translation: 同化是指一个音在邻近音的影响下发生的变化。

structure.text: Assimilation
is
the changing of a sound under the influence of a neighbouring one
under the influence of a neighbouring one

difficult_points: the changing of a sound 是动名词短语作表语，指“某个音发生的变化”。
a neighbouring one 中 one 指代前面的 a sound，即“邻近的另一个音”。

sentences.sentence: For example, the word scant was once skamt, but the /m/ has been changed to /n/ under the influence of the following /t/.
sentences.translation: 例如，scant这个词曾经读作skamt，但由于受到后面/t/音的影响，/m/音已经变成了/n/音。

structure.text: the word scant
was once skamt
but the /m/
has been changed to /n/
under the influence of the following /t/

difficult_points: has been changed to 是现在完成时的被动语态，强调/m/音演变为/n/音这一结果延续至今。
under the influence of the following /t/ 说明音变发生的原因，即受后面/t/音的影响。

sentences.sentence: Greater efficiency has hereby been achieved, because /n/ and /t/are articulated in the same place (with the tip of the tongue against the teeth-ridge), whereas/m/ is articulated elsewhere (with the two lips).
sentences.translation: 由此便实现了更高的发音效率，因为/n/和/t/是在同一个部位发出的（舌尖抵住齿龈），而/m/则是在别处发出的（用双唇）。

structure.text: Greater efficiency
has hereby been achieved
because /n/ and /t/are articulated in the same place
with the tip of the tongue against the teeth-ridge
whereas/m/ is articulated elsewhere
with the two lips

difficult_points: has hereby been achieved 是被动语态，hereby 意为“由此、因此”，承接上一句/m/变为/n/的音变。
because 引导原因状语从句，解释效率提高的原因。
whereas 引导对比状语从句，将/m/的发音部位与/n/、/t/的发音部位进行对比。

sentences.sentence: So the place of articulation of the nas al consonant has been changed to conform with that of the following plosive.
sentences.translation: 因此，这个鼻辅音的发音部位发生了改变，以便与后面爆破音的发音部位保持一致。

structure.text: the place of articulation of the nas al consonant
has been changed
to conform with that of the following plosive

difficult_points: has been changed 是被动语态，强调发音部位“被改变”。
to conform with 是不定式作目的状语，说明改变发音部位的目的。
that 指代前面的 the place of articulation，避免重复。

sentences.sentence: A more recent example of the same kind of thing is the common pronunciation of football as football.
sentences.translation: 同类现象中一个更晚近的例子，是football一词的常见发音被读作football。

structure.text: A more recent example of the same kind of thing
is
the common pronunciation of football as football

difficult_points: the same kind of thing 承接前文的同化现象，指与前述音变相同类型的变化。
原文 football as football 前后重复，疑为誊录讹误，未能准确保留原始的音变示例。

sentences.sentence: Assimilation is not the only way in which we change our pronunciation in order to increase efficiency.
sentences.translation: 同化并不是我们为了提高效率而改变发音的唯一方式。

structure.text: Assimilation
is not the only way
in which we change our pronunciation in order to increase efficiency
in order to increase efficiency

difficult_points: in which 引导定语从句，修饰 the only way，相当于 in this way we change...。
in order to 引导目的状语，说明改变发音的目的。

sentences.sentence: It is very common for consonants to be lost at the end of a word: in Middle English, word-final [-n] was often lost in unstressed syllables, so that baken 'to bake' changed from['ba:kan] to ['ba:k3],and later to [ba:k].
sentences.translation: 辅音在词末脱落是很常见的现象：在中古英语中，非重读音节中的词末[-n]音常常脱落，因此baken（意为“烤”）从['ba:kan]变成了['ba:k3]，后来又变成了[ba:k]。

structure.text: It
is very common
for consonants to be lost at the end of a word
in Middle English
word-final [-n]
was often lost in unstressed syllables
so that baken 'to bake' changed from['ba:kan] to ['ba:k3],and later to [ba:k]

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，真正的主语是 for consonants to be lost... 这一不定式复合结构，for consonants 是不定式的逻辑主语。
冒号后的内容用中古英语的具体例子说明辅音词末脱落这一现象。
so that 引导结果状语从句，说明词末[-n]脱落导致的发音演变结果。
'to bake' 是插入的英文释义，说明 baken 一词的含义。

expressions.text: be lost at the end of a word

sentences.sentence: Consonant-clusters are often simplified.
sentences.translation: 辅音丛也常常被简化。

structure.text: Consonant-clusters
are often simplified

difficult_points: are often simplified 是被动语态，consonant-clusters 指连续出现的多个辅音组合。
often 说明这种简化是常见现象，而非个别特例。

sentences.sentence: At one time there was a [t] in words like castle and Christmas, and an initial [k] in words like knight and know.
sentences.translation: 曾经，castle和Christmas这类词中有一个[t]音，knight和know这类词的词首也有一个[k]音。

structure.text: At one time
there was a [t] in words like castle and Christmas
and an initial [k] in words like knight and know

difficult_points: there was 后省略了并列部分的谓语，and 后完整应为 there was an initial [k]...。
like castle and Christmas 与 like knight and know 分别举例说明[t]和[k]音曾经存在的位置。

sentences.sentence: Sometimes a whole syllable is dropped out when two successive syllables begin with the same consonant (haplology): a recent example is temporary, which in Britain is often pronounced as if it were tempory.
sentences.translation: 有时，当两个连续的音节以相同的辅音开头时，整个音节会被省略掉（即“重叠简化”现象）：一个近期的例子是temporary一词，在英国常被读成好似tempory一样。

structure.text: a whole syllable
is dropped out
when two successive syllables begin with the same consonant
(haplology)
a recent example is temporary
which in Britain is often pronounced as if it were tempory
as if it were tempory

difficult_points: when 引导时间状语从句，说明音节省略发生的条件。
haplology 是语言学术语“重叠简化/异化省略”，是对前述现象的命名。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，修饰 temporary，说明其在英国的实际读法。
as if it were tempory 使用虚拟语气，表示“仿佛……一样”，实际发音已接近tempory但拼写未变。
